summary.intent: Fix GitHub #594 where BOOT.md startup context injection was silently broken — the hook-based approach fired on a read-only GatewayStart event and its output was never consumed. Replace it with the existing workspace markdown pattern (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) so BOOT.md is loaded per-session and injected into the system prompt.
summary.outcome: Successfully replaced the broken boot-md hook with the workspace markdown pattern: added loader functions, threaded boot_text through PromptPersona and all build_system_prompt_* call sites, deleted the hook, updated docs, added tests, and merged main twice with conflict resolution. PR #614 created and pushed.

repo: Workspace markdown files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, MEMORY.md, now BOOT.md) all follow the same pattern: loaded via load_*_md_for_agent() in config/loader.rs, added to PromptPersona in chat/src/lib.rs, injected via append_*_section() in agents/src/prompt.rs
GatewayStart is a read-only event fired once at server start — it cannot be used for per-session context injection
The prompt build functions have both a public wrapper (build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime) and an internal _details variant — both need parameter updates when adding new prompt fields
gemini-3-* models gained reasoning variants via starts_with('gemini-3') check in providers/src/lib.rs, causing the model count test to need updating from 6 to 9 after the Gemini 3.x catalog update
WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS was renamed to DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS and increased from 6000 to 32000 chars during a main branch update merged mid-PR
append_truncated_text_block gained a 'name' parameter and now returns WorkspaceFilePromptStatus after a main branch refactor

code.finding: build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime delegates to build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime_details — both wrappers and both _details variants need boot_text parameter when adding new prompt fields
list_and_list_all_return_all_registered_models hardcodes expected model count — must be updated whenever new reasoning variants are added to any provider
load_*_md_for_agent() functions follow agent-specific path first, fall back to global data_dir() path via or_else — new BOOT.md follows this same fallback pattern

workflow: replace_all edits are effective for threading a new parameter through many call sites with consistent indentation patterns, but indentation variations require separate replace_all calls
Spawning a subagent to handle bulk test call-site updates (20+ locations) keeps the main context clean
Greptile bot catches stale test assertions referencing deleted hook names — worth checking after deleting any registered component
The single uncovered line in Codecov (load_boot_md_for_agent call in persona loader) is acceptable as integration-level code, same as all other workspace markdown fields

friction: Multiple rounds of PR comment fixes required (stale hook test, pre-existing CI model count failure, then clean Greptile pass) — required 3 separate push/fix cycles

Merging main mid-PR introduced a breaking rename (WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS → DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS), a new _details function variant pattern, and a changed append_truncated_text_block signature — required reading large sections of prompt.rs to reconcile
CI failures were mostly cascade failures from a local/E2E status gate system, making it hard to identify the single real test failure (model count assertion) among many dependent job failures
Indentation variations across call sites (3 different levels) meant replace_all could not be used uniformly — required three separate replacement passes
open_items: The load_boot_md_for_agent() call in load_prompt_persona_for_agent() (chat/src/lib.rs) has no unit test coverage — integration-level only, same as all other workspace markdown loaders
The CI local/E2E Validation gate system that caused cascading failures is opaque — unclear what triggers it or how to unblock it without a full environment
